paper_title: OmniInfer: System-Wide Acceleration Techniques for Optimizing LLM Serving Throughput and Latency
paper_summary: OmniInfer is a unified system‑level framework that boosts large‑language‑model (LLM) serving efficiency by coordinating expert placement, cache compression, and request scheduling. It consists of three complementary modules: OmniPlacement, which performs load‑aware Mixture‑of‑Experts (MoE) scheduling; OmniAttn, which accelerates sparse attention operations; and OmniProxy, which introduces disaggregation‑aware request scheduling. Built on top of the vLLM engine, OmniInfer coordinates prefill and decode phases across a cluster, exploiting sparsity and adaptive resource disaggregation. In experiments on the DeepSeek‑R1 model deployed on a 10‑node Ascend 910C cluster, the framework achieved 616 queries per minute, cutting the time‑per‑token (TPOT) by 36% and the time‑to‑first‑token (TTFT) by 38%. The system demonstrates how fine‑grained, cross‑layer optimizations can yield substantial throughput and latency improvements without modifying the underlying model architecture. Its open‑source design invites integration with other serving stacks and hardware back‑ends.
relevance: Efficient LLM serving is critical for real‑time AI applications and for reducing the operational cost of large‑scale deployments. OmniInfer’s holistic approach enables vendors to extract more performance from existing clusters, thereby accelerating time‑to‑market for new models. By combining expert scheduling, sparse attention, and disaggregation, the paper opens avenues for adaptive resource allocation strategies and dynamic scaling policies. Future work could explore hardware‑specific optimizations, automated load prediction, and extending the framework to multimodal models.
